value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-8203-RT7S-D4P8-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR UPDATES: INDUSTRY LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-KX53-RY52-6111-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR UPDATES: INDUSTRY LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR UPDATES: INDUSTRY LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-RKS3-RS4G-S26P-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY & PEER INSIGHTS: TRENDS AND HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJN-93V3-RRRC-30M3-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT UPDATES FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT UPDATES FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Sideris reduces shareholding by 18.2%, transaction valued at $2.9 million Wednesday May 13, 2026

Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) CEO Harry K. Sideris held 120,636 shares in Duke Energy as at Feb 27, 2026. Two months later, Harry K. Sideris held 98,642 shares worth $12.3 million at the close price of $124.54.

This is a decrease of 21,994 shares (18.2%) in his holding over a period.

The Volume Weghted Average Price (VWAP) of DUK in the period Feb 27 to May 11 was $129.68. The value of the transaction is estimated at $2.9 million.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: HARRY K. SIDERIS, CEO AND PRESIDENT

SECTION 2: DUKE ENERGY: SEC FILINGS

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: HARRY K. SIDERIS, CEO AND PRESIDENT

Harry Sideris is president and chief executive officer of Duke Energy, one of the nation's largest energy holding companies. Under Sideris' leadership, Duke Energy is focused on critical infrastructure investments that meet historic load growth, provide superior service to customers and communities, and maintain safety and operational excellence.

Sideris oversees one of the largest regulated capital plans in the industry, supporting energy modernization that delivers value for Duke Energy's customers and shareholders, advances the nation's growth and economic development aspirations and makes progress on the company's climate goals. Duke Energy is executing a diverse generation strategy, adding natural gas assets to its portfolio, extending the life of its nuclear plants, modernizing the energy grid, advocating for new dispatchable technologies, and adding new renewables and energy storage while collaborating with stakeholders and policymakers to advance supportive energy policy.

Duke Energy has paid a quarterly cash dividend on its common stock for 99 consecutive years. The company has also been named to the Forbes list of "America's Best Employers" and recognized by Fortune as one of the "World's Most Admired Companies" in the electric and gas utilities industry.

Before becoming CEO in April 2025, Sideris served as president of Duke Energy, leading the company's electric and natural gas utilities, including all aspects of operations, customer service and delivery, economic development, regulatory and legislative affairs, and grid and generation strategy. A 29-year veteran of the company, Sideris is a long-standing member of Duke Energy's senior management committee. His prior leadership roles include executive vice president of customer experience, solutions and services; president of Duke Energy's utility operations in Florida; and vice president of environmental, health and safety. He began his career at Progress Energy (formerly Carolina Power & Light), prior to the merger with Duke Energy in 2012.

Sideris currently serves on the boards of directors for the Edison Electric Institute, the Institute of Nuclear Power Operations and the World Association of Nuclear Operators. He is also a member of the Business Roundtable and Charlotte Executive Leadership Council.

Sideris holds a Bachelor of Science degree in chemical engineering from North Carolina State University and a Master of Business Administration degree from Campbell University. He also completed the Advanced Management Program at the University of Chicago.

AMERICAN RESULTS

Alico (NASDAQ:ALCO) announced EPS of $1.03 for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, recovered from $-15.79 in the previous corresponding period.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 79.3% from $34.9m to $7.2m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 5.9% to 26.6%

Note:

- EPS of $1.03

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 79.3%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025Revenue, $ million7.234.9PCP growth in Revenue %-798.6

ACTIVITIES:

Alico, Inc., traded on the Nasdaq under the ticker ALCO, is a diversified agribusiness and land management company headquartered in Florida. Its core activities center on agricultural operations, including the production and marketing of citrus fruits, sugarcane, and cattle ranching, alongside land leasing, development, and conservation efforts. The company manages extensive land holdings for farming, water resource management, and environmental stewardship, supporting sustainable practices in the agriculture sector. In recent years, Alico's fastest-growing segments have been its citrus production, driven by increasing demand for fresh and processed citrus products, and its land development initiatives, which include strategic partnerships for renewable energy projects and conservation easements. These areas have fueled revenue growth amid market challenges, positioning Alico as a key player in enhancing food security and environmental sustainability in the southeastern United States. With a commitment to operational efficiency and innovation, Alico continues to adapt to evolving agricultural trends.

MARKET POSITION:

Alico, Inc. (NASDAQ:ALCO), the NASDAQ's 14th largest Food company by market cap, jumped $11.23 (or 38.1%) in the trailing 12 months to May 08, 2026 to close at $40.68. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 47.1% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -9.0%. Average daily volume of 28,503 shares in the trailing year was 0.7 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - MARKET DIGEST: INDUSTRY & PEER UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-FRB3-RRRN-4370-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - MARKET DIGEST: INDUSTRY & PEER UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - MARKET DIGEST: INDUSTRY & PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHER PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-YDG3-RRJ6-N2KW-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT NEWS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJN-FNH3-S0MP-428F-00000-00Interim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Alico reports 79% fall in Half-Yearly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 11 May 2026 23:37 EDT Release Date: May 11, 20262026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisInterim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Alico reports 79% fall in Half-Yearly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 11 May 2026 23:37 EDT Release Date: May 11, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Alico (NASDAQ:ALCO), announced total revenue of $7.2m for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, down 79% from the previous corresponding period.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 79.3% from $34.9m to $7.2m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 5.9% to 26.6%

Note:

- EPS of $1.03

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 79.3%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025Revenue, $ million7.234.9PCP growth in Revenue %-798.6

ACTIVITIES:

Alico, Inc., traded on the Nasdaq under the ticker ALCO, is a diversified agribusiness and land management company headquartered in Florida. Its core activities center on agricultural operations, including the production and marketing of citrus fruits, sugarcane, and cattle ranching, alongside land leasing, development, and conservation efforts. The company manages extensive land holdings for farming, water resource management, and environmental stewardship, supporting sustainable practices in the agriculture sector. In recent years, Alico's fastest-growing segments have been its citrus production, driven by increasing demand for fresh and processed citrus products, and its land development initiatives, which include strategic partnerships for renewable energy projects and conservation easements. These areas have fueled revenue growth amid market challenges, positioning Alico as a key player in enhancing food security and environmental sustainability in the southeastern United States. With a commitment to operational efficiency and innovation, Alico continues to adapt to evolving agricultural trends.

MARKET POSITION:

Alico, Inc. (NASDAQ:ALCO), the NASDAQ's 14th largest Food company by market cap, jumped $11.23 (or 38.1%) in the trailing 12 months to May 08, 2026 to close at $40.68. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 47.1% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -9.0%. Average daily volume of 28,503 shares in the trailing year was 0.7 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: RECENT TRENDS AND KEY HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-MN03-RXCM-3007-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: RECENT TRENDS AND KEY HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: RECENT TRENDS AND KEY HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Duke Energy Could Consider Advantages From NRG Energy Branding Allegations Against NextNRG Entities *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-4V63-RY9N-X0HM-00000-00Duke Energy Could Consider Advantages From NRG Energy Branding Allegations Against NextNRG Entities *Updated*2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisDuke Energy Could Consider Advantages From NRG Energy Branding Allegations Against NextNRG Entities *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

This situation emphasizes potential vulnerabilities for established energy brands when new market entrants adopt similar terminology in the renewable energy and electric vehicle infrastructure sectors. Legal determinations may affect how courts assess consumer confusion within sector-specific naming conventions, which could increase compliance expenditures related to brand monitoring across power generation and clean energy markets. Wider industry implications involve increased attention to intellectual property within an aggregating market environment, where distinct differentiation assists with customer retention and investor assurance during regulatory adjustments toward sustainable options. Proper administration can mitigate brand dilution while supporting existing market positioning.

Background of the Case

NRG Energy, Inc. initiated legal action on April 18, 2025 (Case No. 4:2025cv01790, S.D. Tex., Judge Lee H. Rosenthal) against NextNRG Holding Corp and associated parties (including NextNRG LLC, NextCharging LLC, and affiliated Miami-based entities previously connected to EzFill Holdings, Inc., which underwent rebranding in February 2025). The legal filing asserts trademark infringement according to 15 U.S.C. § 1114 (Lanham Act), contending the defendants' utilization of "NEXT NRG" and related identifiers in EV charging, microgrids, artificial intelligence-supported renewables, and energy services generates a likelihood of confusion with NRG's established family of trademarks. NRG requests a permanent injunction, damages (including potential trebling for deliberate infringement), profits, and attorney compensation. The proceedings are currently in preliminary pre-trial phases with a jury request.

Competitive Impacts on Duke Energy

Duke Energy, characterized by established regulated utility operations and a regional footprint, utilizes existing brand equity in power delivery and customer services. The ongoing legal proceedings may elevate general industry cognizance of naming vulnerabilities in diversified portfolios, enabling utilities with recognized regional identities to consolidate relationships across retail electricity and generation segments. This environment could facilitate consistent market positioning during competitive bidding processes and customer selection scenarios.

Possible Opportunities for Duke Energy

Bolstered regional customer loyalty through reinforced brand programs.

Expansion opportunities in adjacent services with protected naming.

Greater investor appeal highlighting operational stability.

Strategic alliances that emphasize compliance and differentiation excellence.

Why This Case Is Challenging for Duke Energy

Secondary impacts from the sector's focus on trademarks might increase diligence obligations for subsidiary branding, promotional materials, and new project identifiers, notably in regulated environments involving public utility commissions. This requirement could add to compliance-related administrative costs and necessitate supplemental attention to brand integrity topics during earnings presentations or regulatory submissions.

Key Challenges for Duke Energy

Heightened reviews for branding across regulated and competitive operations.

Considerations from public utility commissions on naming practices.

Increased costs associated with marketing compliance activities.

Benchmarking against peers on intellectual property standards.

Duke Energy's Strategic Responses

Perform audits of existing trademarks and service marks.

Provide training to teams on trademark best practices.

Engage specialized external counsel for guidance.

Communicate governance and brand strengths to stakeholders.

Key Measures:

A phased implementation supported by oversight committees helps maintain continuity while embedding stronger protections across the organization.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share increase in core service territories.

Additional revenue potential from trusted customer services.

Enhanced perceptions in ESG and regulatory evaluations.

Smoother development cycles for infrastructure projects.

Potential Losses

Compliance investments up to relevant figures.

Resource allocation strain on legal and communications teams.

Potential delays in initiative rollout timelines.

Indirect sector cost impacts that may affect overall margins.

Final Thoughts

Duke Energy maintains a functional position to manage the consequences of this legal dispute by utilizing its regional infrastructure and prioritizing systematic brand administration. This operational method adapts wider industry conditions into differentiation strategies, facilitating consistent organizational development and customer assurance throughout the transition toward sustainable energy infrastructure.

Activities

NRG Energy, Inc., a leading integrated power company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in the generation, transmission, and retail supply of electricity across the United States. The company's core activities include operating a diverse portfolio of power plants fueled by natural gas, coal, nuclear, and renewables, while also providing energy services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers. NRG emphasizes innovative retail solutions, such as demand response programs and energy management tools, to enhance efficiency and customer engagement. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support for clean energy and market demand for sustainable options. Additionally, the company's distributed energy resources, including battery storage and microgrids, are gaining momentum, driven by advancements in technology and the shift toward decentralized power systems. This strategic focus positions NRG for continued growth in a competitive and evolving energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) stands as a prominent player in the integrated power generation and retail electricity industry. The company is actively engaged in the NRG Energy unsecured notes offering and NRG Energy secured notes offering, which are part of its strategic capital growth initiatives. The recent NRG notes offering is a testament to their commitment to ensuring liquidity and operational flexibility. NRG Energy notes offering, focusing on both NRG Energy bond offering and NRG bonds offering, signifies their robust financing strategy. With the NRG Energy debt offering, NRG Energy notes issuance, and NRG Energy bond issuance, they are well-positioned to meet market challenges. Furthermore, the NRG Energy debt securities offering and NRG Energy corporate bond offering cater to diverse investor interests. Announcements such as NRG Energy investor relations notes and NRG Energy press release notes offering demonstrate transparency. These activities, found in NRG Energy SEC filing notes offering and NRG Energy 8-K notes offering, ensure stakeholders remain well-informed. The company’s structured offerings, including NRG Energy prospectus unsecured notes and NRG Energy prospectus secured notes, solidify its reputation. Through NRG Energy senior unsecured notes, NRG Energy senior secured notes, and flexible options like NRG Energy fixed-rate notes offering, NRG Energy floating-rate notes offering, they engage in comprehensive financial planning. With the NRG Energy note sale announcement and NRG Energy capital markets transaction, NRG Energy underscores its adaptive policy. Their engagement in NRG Energy debt financing and analysis of NRG Energy credit rating impact showcase its strategic foresight. Being proactive with NYSE:NRG notes offering and NRG note offering news signifies market alertness. By regularly issuing NRG Energy bond prospectus and tailoring NRG Energy institutional notes offering and NRG Energy retail notes offering, NRG Energy positions itself as a compelling choice for both retail and institutional investors.

value.Title: Celebrate Earth With These 2 Unstoppable Green Energy Stocks

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ9-1BX3-S79W-R329-00000-00Celebrate Earth With These 2 Unstoppable Green Energy Stocks2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisCelebrate Earth With These 2 Unstoppable Green Energy Stocks

newsfeedback@fool.com (Reuben Gregg Brewer)

May 10th, 2026 ( The Motley Fool - Delivered by Newstex )

Key Points

Brookfield Renewable provides broad exposure to green energy and offers a large and growing dividend.

NextEra Energy's solar and wind business is among the largest in the world.

Oil prices are making headlines, but don't get caught up in what is likely to be a transitory price swing. The world is still shifting toward cleaner energy options. Given the green energy sector's still small size, there are material growth opportunities ahead for investors. Two great options that let you lean into the growing importance of clean energy on planet Earth are Brookfield Renewable (NYSE: BEP)(NYSE: BEPC) and NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE). Here's a look at each one.

Brookfield Renewable has all of your bases covered

Brookfield Renewable's portfolio spans across North America, South America, Europe, and Asia. It generates electricity via hydroelectric, solar, and wind systems. And it provides energy storage and nuclear power services, as well. It is a simple and easy way to add green energy investments to your portfolio.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire?Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue

Image source: Getty Images.

It is also a reliable high-yield income investment. The distribution has been increased regularly for a decade at an annualized rate of 5%. The goal is to continue increasing the distribution at a rate of 5% to 9% per year. Backing that is management's projection for 10% funds from operation growth through at least 2031. The goal is to invest up to $10 billion in growth over that span.

The yield is currently 4.7% for the partnership units and 4.4% for the corporate shares. They represent the same entity; the yield difference is due to higher demand for the corporate shares. While institutional investors may not be allowed to buy partnerships, there's no particular reason why smaller investors should avoid the higher-yielding partnership units.

NextEra Energy gets you halfway in

NextEra Energy is one of the world's biggest utilities, operating a large regulated utility in Florida. However, there's another side to the business. The company is also one of the world's largest producers of solar and wind power. The utility is a slow-and-steady foundation, while the clean energy business is the company's growth engine. The company is working on a backlog of 20 gigawatts of clean energy projects, but hopes to grow its backlog to as much as 100 gigawatts by 2032.

Management is projecting earnings growth of 8% a year through 2035. That supports the near-term goal of 6% dividend growth through 2028, but likely means that the multi-decade dividend streak will continue well past that. The dividend yield is a well above market 2.6%.

Have your green energy and collect some green along the way

The growth of the clean energy sector will take place over decades. Brookfield Renewable and NextEra Energy are already industry leaders. Still, buying them today will let you collect attractive and growing dividends while continuing to benefit from the long-term green energy transition. Now that's something to celebrate if you are a dividend investor.

Reuben Gregg Brewer has positions in Brookfield Renewable Partners. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends NextEra Energy. The Motley Fool recommends Brookfield Renewable and Brookfield Renewable Partners. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy.

urn:contentItem:6JJ2-HSG3-S8KY-F32J-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website
