value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY NEWS RECAP

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ5-CT13-RRWC-407K-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY NEWS RECAP2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY NEWS RECAP

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N21S-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT INSIGHTS: SECTOR TRENDS & NEWS DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: FH Capital Takes Majority Stake in JinkoSolar (U.S.)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-7BN3-RX2W-P0HT-00000-00FH Capital Takes Majority Stake in JinkoSolar (U.S.)2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisFH Capital Takes Majority Stake in JinkoSolar (U.S.)by Polina Kozareva

FH Capital on Friday, May 8, said it will acquire a 75.1% stake in Jinko Solar (U.S.) Industries Inc., the North American subsidiary of JinkoSolar Holding Co. Ltd. (JKS).Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates-headquartered FH Capital didn't disclose the financial details of the transaction, but JinkoSolar in a regulatory filing said it will receive roughly $191.5 million in three tranches.JinkoSolar, which will retain a 24.9% minority interest, said the sale is part of a strategic restructuring of its overseas business and assets because of an uncertain global macroeconomic environment.FH Capital said the transaction will give it a diversified U.S. clean energy manufacturing platform aligned with rising demand and evolving energy policy.The transaction transfers control of a 2-gigawatt solar module manufacturing facility and a growing battery energy storage systems, or BESS, business.

FH Capital intends to invest in expanding solar production capacity and launching domestic BESS manufacturing.Founded in 2017, Jacksonville, Fla.-based Jinko Solar (U.S.) is the domestic manufacturing arm for Shangrao, China-based Jinko Solar, one of the world's largest solar module producers. The subsidiary operates a fully automated assembly facility that produces high-efficiency modules to meet the growing demand for renewable energy across North America.Founded in 2004 as a subsidiary of Finance House, FH Capital is a private equity and investment firm that focuses on growth-oriented clean energy and net zero economy investments. It combines deep sector expertise with financial resources to provide asset management, investment banking and infrastructure financing for companies scaling in the renewable energy sector.The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.Elsewhere in the segment this month, Mars Equity Partners holding Mars Energy Group acquired Solar Savings Direct for an undisclosed amount. In April, Hamilton Lane Inc. (HLNE) and Grosvenor Capital Management acquired a 49.9% stake in Big Sky Wind Farm from Power Sustainable Capital holding Potentia Renewables Inc. and Headwater Energy purchased Arena Renewables for an undisclosed amount.JinkoSolar relied on Morgan Stanley Asia Ltd. for financial advice and on Denise Shiu, Shuang Zhao and Mengshan Xu of Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton LLP in Hong Kong for counsel.FH Capital tapped Latham & Watkins LLP for counsel, while managing partner Sanjeev Chaurasia worked on the acquisition in-house.

DEAL SIZE

$ 50-250 Million

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2T4-W287-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-2061-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-SVN3-S56J-J2HR-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-MPX3-S7MH-6389-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGY-P943-RVSH-W074-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - HIGHLIGHTS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEER COMPANIES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-KN63-RX6V-N010-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - HIGHLIGHTS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEER COMPANIES2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - HIGHLIGHTS FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEER COMPANIES

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA [4 Press Releases]

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO NORTH EUROPE

Please find the new applicable CMA CGM Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates (*) as follows as from May 15th, 2026 (loading date) until further notice:

20'GP 40'GP/40'HC/40'REEFER Asia to North Europe USD 1,950 USD 3,500

Base port to base port rates. Out-ports are subject to usual TAO/TAD.

These new FAK rates will apply as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian ports (including Japan, South East Asia & Bangladesh) - Destination Range: To all North European ports (including UK and the full range from Portugal to Finland/Estonia) - Cargo: Dry, OOG, Paying empties & Reefer - Date of application: From May 15th, 2026 (date of loading in the origin ports) until further notice

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: PSS - FROM EAST MEDITERRANEAN TO USA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient services, CMA CGM Group wishes to inform of the following Peak Season Surcharge (PSS)* : From May 15th, 2026 until further notice:

- Origin range: From East Mediterranean including Turkiye, Greece, Lebanon, Bulgaria, Egypt, Syria, Georgia, Ukraine, Romania, Croatia, Albania, Slovenia

- Destination range: To the US East Coast, US West Coast, US Gulf Coast and all inland destinations reached via said ports

- Cargo: all except OOG

- Amounts: USD 1,050 per 20' | USD 2,100 per 40'/40'HC/45'

Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: FAK RATES - FROM ASIA TO THE MEDITERRANEAN & NORTH AFRICA

In a continued effort to provide our customers with reliable and efficient service, CMA CGM announces an increase on our Freight All Kind (FAK) rates as follows as from May 15th to 31st, 2026.

20' 40'/40' HC WEST MED USD 3,300 USD 4,600 ADRIATIC USD 3,400 USD 4,700 EAST MED USD 3,500 USD 4,800 BLACK SEA USD 3,500 USD 4,800 ALGERIA USD 5,100 USD 7,200

These new FAK rates will be applied as follows:

- Origin Range: From all Asian main ports

- Destination range: To all Mediterranean ports (areas mentioned above)

- Cargo: Dry, Reefer, OOG, Paying Empties

- Date of application: May 15th to 31st, 2026 Source: Company Website

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA: BAF UPDATE - TUNISIA RORO SERVICE

Following bunker prices evolution since the last surcharge adjustment, CMA CGM SSL Intra Europe will adjust BAF as follows on its Roro South service connecting France with Tunisia (import & export): Effective May 1st, 2026: Cargo RORO Tunisia service Roll, full EUR 649/unit Roll, empty EUR 430/unit Other rolling units* EUR 73/LM Pass. cars, minivans EUR 248/unit Breakbulk EUR 41/CBM

(*) Truck/Earth moving equipment, on wheels or tracks, duty trucks and agricultural devices, livestock trucks

For any questions or additional information, please contact your local CMA CGM office.

Source: Company Website

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [4 Press Releases]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME: AMERICAN ENERGY LNG CARRIER TRANSPORTS MORE THAN 500 MILLION GALLONS TO PUERTO RICO IN FIRST YEAR

Crowley marked one year of operations of American Energy, the first U.S.-flagged liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier dedicated to serving Puerto Rico. This milestone highlights the company's commitment to reliably providing LNG supply to support the island's energy resilience.

Source: Company Website

+ NEW CROWLEY BUSINESS STRUCTURE ACCELERATES GROWTH WITH FOWLER AND FENCIL AS DIVISION PRESIDENTS

James Fowler is named executive vice president and division president, Shipping and Logistics.

Kollin Fencil is named executive vice president and division president, Energy.

Business lines are consolidated in unified divisions for streamlined operations and customer value.

Crowley announced today a strategic consolidation of its business structure to create more efficiency and value for customers. The company is creating two divisions: shipping and logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and energy, led by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

Source: Company Website

+ CROWLEY EARNS ENVIRONMENTAL ACHIEVEMENT HONORS FOR COMBINED 958 YEARS OF SAFE OPERATIONS

Crowley has been recognized by the Chamber of Shipping of America (CSA) for its commitment to environmental stewardship, earning Environmental Achievement Awards for 108 Crowley-operated vessels that have collectively earned 958 years without an incident.

The awards celebrate companies and mariners who maintain the highest standards of safety and ecological protection. Crowley's achievement reflects its dedication to operational excellence across a diverse owned and managed fleet around the world.

"This recognition by the Chamber of Shipping of America is a powerful reflection of Crowley's unwavering focus on both operational safety and responsible stewardship," said Tucker Gilliam, vice president of fleet operations for Crowley Shipping. "This achievement celebrates our world-class mariners and shoreside operations teams, whose vigilance, professionalism and daily drive to uphold the highest standards are truly what deliver on this promise."

Source: Company Website

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: HARRY K. SIDERIS, CEO AND PRESIDENT

SECTION 2: DUKE ENERGY: SEC FILINGS

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: HARRY K. SIDERIS, CEO AND PRESIDENT

Harry Sideris is president and chief executive officer of Duke Energy, one of the nation's largest energy holding companies. Under Sideris' leadership, Duke Energy is focused on critical infrastructure investments that meet historic load growth, provide superior service to customers and communities, and maintain safety and operational excellence.

Sideris oversees one of the largest regulated capital plans in the industry, supporting energy modernization that delivers value for Duke Energy's customers and shareholders, advances the nation's growth and economic development aspirations and makes progress on the company's climate goals. Duke Energy is executing a diverse generation strategy, adding natural gas assets to its portfolio, extending the life of its nuclear plants, modernizing the energy grid, advocating for new dispatchable technologies, and adding new renewables and energy storage while collaborating with stakeholders and policymakers to advance supportive energy policy.

Duke Energy has paid a quarterly cash dividend on its common stock for 99 consecutive years. The company has also been named to the Forbes list of "America's Best Employers" and recognized by Fortune as one of the "World's Most Admired Companies" in the electric and gas utilities industry.

Before becoming CEO in April 2025, Sideris served as president of Duke Energy, leading the company's electric and natural gas utilities, including all aspects of operations, customer service and delivery, economic development, regulatory and legislative affairs, and grid and generation strategy. A 29-year veteran of the company, Sideris is a long-standing member of Duke Energy's senior management committee. His prior leadership roles include executive vice president of customer experience, solutions and services; president of Duke Energy's utility operations in Florida; and vice president of environmental, health and safety. He began his career at Progress Energy (formerly Carolina Power & Light), prior to the merger with Duke Energy in 2012.

Sideris currently serves on the boards of directors for the Edison Electric Institute, the Institute of Nuclear Power Operations and the World Association of Nuclear Operators. He is also a member of the Business Roundtable and Charlotte Executive Leadership Council.

Sideris holds a Bachelor of Science degree in chemical engineering from North Carolina State University and a Master of Business Administration degree from Campbell University. He also completed the Advanced Management Program at the University of Chicago.

CLEARWAY ENERGY QUARTERLY DIVIDENDS UP 1.6% SEQUENTIALLY FROM PREVIOUS QUARTER

Clearway Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 46.76c per share for Q1/2026; up 1.6% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 46.02c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, June 01, 2026 and the record date is Monday, June 01, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, June 15. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended May 12 were $1.80. This reflects actual dividend yield of 4.7% at the last price of $38.51.

Clearway Energy, Inc., a publicly traded company on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading renewable energy firm focused on the development, ownership, and operation of clean energy assets. Its core business activities encompass the generation of electricity from a mix of wind, solar, and natural gas facilities, emphasizing sustainable practices to meet growing energy demands. The company's fastest-growing segments include utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to favorable regulatory environments, technological advancements, and increasing corporate commitments to decarbonization. By leveraging strategic partnerships and efficient operations, Clearway enhances its portfolio to deliver reliable, low-carbon power solutions, positioning itself as a key player in the transition to a greener energy landscape.

Duke Energy Corporation is a leading U.S.-based energy company, traded on the New York Stock Exchange, that specializes in the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, as well as natural gas services. Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, the company serves approximately 8 million customers across six states, primarily through its regulated utilities in the Carolinas, Florida, and the Midwest. Its core operations focus on reliable power supply from a diverse mix of sources, including coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewables. The fastest-growing segments include renewable energy initiatives, such as solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and electric vehicle charging infrastructure are driving growth, positioning Duke Energy as a key player in the transition to a more sustainable energy future.

Republic Services, Inc., a prominent player on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of environmental services in the United States, specializing in non-hazardous solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal. The company's core operations encompass a comprehensive network of landfills, transfer stations, and recycling facilities, serving residential, commercial, and industrial clients across the nation. Its business is underpinned by a strong focus on sustainability, including innovative waste-to-energy initiatives that convert landfill gas into renewable energy. The fastest-growing segments include recycling services and environmental solutions, fueled by rising demand for eco-friendly practices, regulatory mandates, and corporate sustainability goals. Through strategic acquisitions and operational efficiencies, Republic Services continues to expand its footprint, positioning itself as a key contributor to resource recovery and circular economy efforts while maintaining high standards of safety and compliance.

value.Title: Jenn Pellegrino: Florida cannot afford to lose the Fort Meade data center fight

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-K4C3-RWWG-D3PF-00000-00Jenn Pellegrino: Florida cannot afford to lose the Fort Meade data center fight2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:39ZLexisNexisJenn Pellegrino: Florida cannot afford to lose the Fort Meade data center fight
