Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Guest Author

May 14th, 2026 ( Florida Politics - Delivered by Newstex )

President Donald J. Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge lays out a clear, common-sense standard: America will dominate the AI race against China, and working families will not be left to finance the profits of big corporations.

That is the essence of America First leadership.

And there is no more consequential place to prove it than in Trump's state.

Florida is not just another red state. What happens here sends a signal to every Governor, every County Commission, and every state Legislature watching from across the country. If Florida can build AI infrastructure the right way, accountable, community-focused, and protective of ratepayers, it proves the blueprint works.

If Florida fumbles it, opponents of American leadership in AI will use that failure as a weapon everywhere else.

The Fort Meade project in Polk County is a critical concrete test. The proposed $2.6 billion project would be Florida's first facility of its kind and a major investment in the state.Aside from creating well-paying jobs, it would generatehundreds of millions of dollars in revenue for the city, reducing the burden on taxpayers and helping build vital infrastructure.The developer has also reduced projected water usage through a closed-loop cooling system, exactly the kind of environmental accountability that earns public trust.

Fort Meade Mayor Jaret Landon Williams has shown leadership, transparency, and integrity on this issue, holding open town halls, demanding accountability from the developer, and putting the community's interests first throughout.

As a result, the City Commissioners unanimously approved the development agreement. These are local leaders doing the hard work for their communities.

Yet the same forces working to kill data center development in blue states have arrived in Florida. Leading the legal campaign against the project is Earthjustice, a California-based organization founded as the Sierra Club Legal Defense Fund in 1971.

Also involved is Food and Water Watch, a group so extreme that they've attacked the Environmental Defense Fund (EDF) as 'shilling' for corporate interests. Food and Water Watch campaigns against nuclear energy, conventional fuels, and conventional farming techniques, and advocates for an immediate transition to 100% weather-dependent renewable energy.

To these extreme groups, Fort Meade is just a battlefield in a much larger campaign to block American AI infrastructure everywhere.

They are attempting to implement the agenda of Democratic Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York, who have already introduced the AI Data Center Moratorium Act to impose a nationwide ban on new facilities. These are radical positions that certainly don't represent Florida.

Congressman Byron Donalds, who is running for Governor of Florida, has been supporting Trump's plan, which would advance economic growth while being clear that 'these data center projects are going to have to prove that they're not going to take away from the power grid, that they're not going to raise utility prices on Florida consumers.'

The path forward is difficult but straightforward. Data centers built with genuine community engagement, transparent rate structures, real environmental accountability, and on-site power solutions will earn public support and deserve it.

The Fort Meade project, with its job creation, infrastructure commitments, and reduced environmental footprint, represents that standard. Florida's leaders need to hold the line against a well-funded and ideologically coordinated campaign to shut it down and put Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge into practice.

The rest of the country is watching. We can't afford for Florida to let radical environmental groups, instead of local leaders, dictate the economic future of Polk County.

___

Jenn Pellegrino is the founder of Defend Forgotten America Action, which seeks to champion forgotten communities and restore power to the people who built America. Jenn previously served as chief spokesperson for the America First Policy Institute, a primetime host for Newsmax, and a White House Correspondent during Trump's first term.

The post Jenn Pellegrino: Florida cannot afford to lose the Fort Meade data center fight appeared first on Florida Politics - Campaigns & Elections. Lobbying & Government..

Link to the original story.

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 12:04 EDT

On 5th May 2026, Duke Energy Corporation (NYSE:DUK) revised its fair value from US$139.82 to US$139.39, a modest adjustment that aligns with a more conservative outlook amid rising high US$130s targets and a strong share run since early December. The move reflects a shift in valuation while the stock performance remains firm and analysts lift long-term expectations.

SECTION 1 DUKE ENERGY RECENT PRESS RELEASES

January 08: Duke Energy quarterly dividends steady

Duke Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of $1.06. The ex-dividend date is Friday, February 13, 2026 and the record date is Friday, February 13, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 16.

January 08: Duke Energy quarterly dividends steady

Duke Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of $1.06. The ex-dividend date is Friday, February 13, 2026 and the record date is Friday, February 13, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, March 16.

November 13, 2025: Duke Energy quarterly dividends steady

Duke Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of $1.06 per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of $1.06. The ex-dividend date is Friday, November 14, 2025 and it is payable on Tuesday, December 16. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended November 13 were $4.20.

SECTION 2 DUKE ENERGY ACTIVITIES

Activities

Duke Energy Corporation is a leading U.S.-based energy company, traded on the New York Stock Exchange, that specializes in the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, as well as natural gas services. Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, the company serves approximately 8 million customers across six states, primarily through its regulated utilities in the Carolinas, Florida, and the Midwest. Its core operations focus on reliable power supply from a diverse mix of sources, including coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewables. The fastest-growing segments include renewable energy initiatives, such as solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and electric vehicle charging infrastructure are driving growth, positioning Duke Energy as a key player in the transition to a more sustainable energy future. It is the NYSE's largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

urn:contentItem:6JGY-HND3-SF12-1046-00000-00Declarations2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:39ZLexisNexisDeclarations

AI Law

"We are telling our clients: You should proceed with caution here."

- Alexandria Gutiérrez Swette, a lawyer at New York-based law firm Kobre & Kim, commenting on a ruling by a federal judge in New York stating that the former CEO of a bankrupt financial services company could not shield his AI chats from prosecutors pursuing securities fraud charges against him. In the wake of the ruling, attorneys have been advising that conversations with chatbots like Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's ChatGPT could be demanded by prosecutors in criminal cases or by litigation adversaries in civil cases.

Solar Farmland

"Many communities want to decarbonize and probably theoretically support renewable energy. When it's your community and your backyard, balancing these processes so people feel like they've had a say without creating so many veto points that nothing can get done, I think, is the trick. And it's not easy to do."

- Juniper Katz, an assistant professor at the University of Massachusetts who focuses on environmental policy, discussing the push/pull between landowners and local governments regarding renewable energy projects. A 2025 Columbia University study found that from 2023 to 2024, there was a 16% increase in local laws across 44 states restricting such projects.

Protecting Mobile Homes

"If it's 1994 or newer, you have options. If it's older, there really aren't many,"

- John Gardner, principal at Lee County Insurance Agency and one of the top writers of mobile home coverage in Florida. Insurance agents and others in the state agreed that, 24 months after St. Petersburg-headquartered American Mobile began canceling policies, finding coverage for many mobile and manufactured homes remains difficult and eye-poppingly expensive. The average mobile home annual premium in parts of southwest Florida is now about $5,000-more than that for some non-mobile, single-family dwellings in a comparable area.

E-Bike Claims Quadruple

"Sometimes, what looks like an e-bike or is marketed as an e-bike is not a bike at all. We are seeing a surge of safety incidents on our sidewalks, parks, and streets. Bike riders and parents: If your or your teen's electric two-wheeled vehicle goes too fast, it might be a motorcycle or a moped-not an e-bike."

- California Attorney General Rob Bonta on e-bike risks. The popularity of e-bikes has led to a rise in claims involving battery-related fire risks and rider injuries. The number of claims quadrupled from roughly 1,000 claims in 2021 to more than 4,000 claims in 2025, a Verisk report found.

NYC Insurance Plan

"The skyrocketing cost of insurance is putting affordable, rent-stabilized housing at risk and risks setting back our efforts to build a more affordable city. This groundbreaking effort will use the City's purchasing power to lower insurance premiums, helping our own investments in affordable housing go farther and reducing operating costs for owners of rent stabilized housing. This is just one step in how we're working to bring down housing costs across the board."

- Leila Bozorg, New York City deputy mayor for Housing and Planning, on a plan to lower the cost of property and liability insurance for affordable and rent-stabilized housing through a city-run insurance program by 2027.

Omaha Sinkholes

"It's great for growing corn, but terrible for building roads."

- Omaha, Nebraska, City Engineer Austin Rowser on sinkhole hazards. Much of Omaha sits atop a fine-grained sediment called loess, which can be easily carried away by water, leaving behind gaps underground. Omaha's sinkholes are generally shallow and often result from human-made infrastructure interacting with the fine-grained sediment. Reported cave-ins have decreased in recent years from more than 500 in 2021 to about 340 last year, but as Omaha's underground infrastructure ages, it could put the city at greater risk of sinkholes, geologists said.

value.Title: Africa 's cellphone towers turn to solar as diesel costs surge
value.Overview: The Independent (United Kingdom), WIRELESS INDUSTRY NEWS, Africa news, Nigeria news, Iran news, South Africa news & MIDDLE EAST NEWS, 1210words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-G2C3-RWNG-D110-00000-00Africa's cellphone towers turn to solar as diesel costs surge2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:47ZLexisNexisWIRELESS INDUSTRY NEWS, Africa news, Nigeria news, Iran news, South Africa news & MIDDLE EAST NEWSAfrica's cellphone towers turn to solar as diesel costs surgeAllan Olingo

Rising diesel prices linked to the Iran war are adding urgency to a shift already underway across Africa to move cellphone towers off fossil fuels and onto solar power.

Diesel, which powers the majority of Africa's roughly 500,000 telecommunications towers, has become more expensive and sometimes harder to secure in recent weeks as global fuel markets tightened following the conflict. Several African countries that rely heavily on imported fuel have reported price spikes and supply disruptions, prompting governments and companies to reassess energy strategies.

The transition to cleaner power for the towers that provide cellphone service predates the latest price shocks, driven by cost pressures and climate goals. But the war in the Middle East has accelerated the push.

"Diesel has always been a major cost, but recent global events have made it even more volatile," said Lande Abudu, senior energy specialist for Africa at GSMA, a global industry organization representing mobile operators. "That strengthens the case for solar and hybrid solutions."

Across the continent, mobile network operators are increasingly adopting hybrid systems that combine solar panels, battery storage and limited diesel backup. Some are aiming for fully solar-powered sites over time, particularly in rural and off-grid areas where extending electricity grids is costly.

Cellphone towers in Africa have traditionally been powered by diesel generators, often large industrial units that require operators to manually refuel them. In contrast, solar-powered towers rely on sunlight, using solar panels to generate electricity and battery systems to store energy for continuous operation.

Globally, most telecommunication towers in developed markets such as the United States and India are connected to the electricity grid, with diesel generators used mainly as backup during outages. In parts of Southeast Asia, including Indonesia, operators have also relied heavily on diesel but are now pursuing similar transitions to renewable energy.

Last month, American-owned Atlas Tower Kenya said it was investing $52.5 million to construct 300 new, solar-powered telecommunication towers to serve major operators, including Safaricom, Airtel and Telkom Kenya. The firm currently has 82% of its 500 towers solar powered.

"It's a significant shift, but there's still a long way to go," said Abudu.

Financial arguments for solar-powered towers

The economics are becoming increasingly compelling. Energy accounts for up to 60% of operating costs for telecom towers in off-grid areas. Diesel, once the default solution, has become more expensive and harder to manage, with challenges ranging from transport logistics to theft and maintenance.

Vodacom Africa said its energy costs rose 5% to $300 million in 2025 from the prior year, citing higher electricity tariffs and fuel prices. The company operates in Egypt, South Africa, Congo, Mozambique, Tanzania and Lesotho, and owns subsidiaries in Kenya and Ethiopia through Safaricom. Safaricom last year raised $153.6 million in green bonds to help it transition its towers to solar power.

In countries like Nigeria, cost pressures have shifted sharply. The removal of fuel subsidies in 2023 triggered a surge in diesel prices, rising by as much as 200% within a year, and operators now spend $400 million annually to keep towers running. Recent global price increases tied to the Iran conflict have added further pressure.

Companies are responding by accelerating clean energy deployments. Firms such as iSAT Africa are rolling out solar-powered towers backed by new financing models, while major operators including Orange, Vodacom, MTN Group and Airtel Africa are expanding solar and hybrid systems across their networks.

"By replacing diesel-powered telecom towers with fully solar-powered infrastructure, we expect to reduce the carbon emissions associated with mobile network operations," said iSAT Africa CEO Rakesh Kukreja in March, announcing new funding for clean energy tower projects.

Early results show significant savings. MTN operations in South Sudan have cut fuel spending by about 30% after adopting solar power, while Airtel Africa, working with ENGIE Energy Access, has reduced diesel use by more than half at sites in Zambia and Congo.

Vodacom Africa said in its 2025 sustainability report that connecting towers to national grids and rolling out more solar and battery were key parts of its strategy to replace diesel generators.

Improving network reliability

The benefits go beyond cost. Solar-powered towers are less vulnerable to fuel shortages and generator failures, improving reliability in underserved areas. In parts of northern Nigeria and Congo, cellular outages linked to fuel shortages predating the war have caused disruptions to services ranging from mobile money transactions to emergency communications.

Replacing diesel generators with solar energy could help close Africa's connectivity gap, where about 65% of people who could benefit from mobile internet remain unconnected, according to the GSMA.

"Renewable energy systems enable faster and more cost-effective expansion into underserved areas," Abudu said.

In rural Kenya, residents in some off-grid communities say solar-powered towers have improved service stability, enabling more consistent access to mobile banking, education and health information.

"Before this telecommunication mast was installed, we struggled to process mobile money payment or even call for help during medical emergencies," said Martin Imwatok, a teacher in northern Kenya. "When these towers go off, business and life stop."

Africa's reliance on diesel is more pronounced due to weaker grid infrastructure, making the shift both more challenging and potentially more transformative. However, regulators say the transition could deliver broader benefits. In Nigeria, the telecom regulator has encouraged operators to integrate towers into solar minigrids that can also supply nearby communities.

"These telecom towers can act as anchor clients for solar minigrids, supplying electricity not only to the towers but also to nearby homes, businesses and public services," said Aminu Maida, head of the Nigerian Communications Commission.

As fuel prices remain uncertain amid global tensions, industry experts say the case for cleaner energy will only grow stronger.

"This is no longer just about climate," Abudu said. "It's about resilience, cost and keeping Africa connected."

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. closed higher the previous trading day. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, increased 0.3% during its previous trading session to close at $94.85.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. closed higher the previous trading day. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, increased 0.3% during its previous trading session to close at $94.85.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. closed lower the previous trading day. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, decreased 0.3% during its previous trading session to close at $94.59.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. closed lower the previous trading day. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, decreased 0.3% during its previous trading session to close at $94.59.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

JUNO BEACH, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Nextera Energy, Inc. closed higher the previous trading day. Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, lifted 1.9% during its previous trading session to close at $94.84.

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a leading energy company, recognized for its expansive renewable energy projects, including solar and wind farms that form the backbone of its diversified energy portfolio. Through subsidiaries like NextEra Energy Services, the company offers cutting-edge renewable solutions, enhancing its commitment to clean energy investments and ESG renewable strategies. Pioneering in AI-driven power solutions, its innovation pipeline focuses on grid modernization and smart grid solutions, underpinning its capacity expansion for AI demand. With utility-scale solar and energy storage for grid advances, NextEra Energy maintains a competitive edge in sustainable infrastructure projects and utility services.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 344 words

Tue May 5, 2026: NextEra Energy's Strategic Shift Towards Gas and Nuclear Fuels

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is positioning itself as a key player in the evolving energy landscape, particularly in light of the 2026 'Reliability Shock.' On the 5th of May 2026, it was reported that NextEra Energy is set to finalize agreements within approximately three months for two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, which are designed to support the burgeoning demand from data centers.

These upcoming projects, strategically located in Pennsylvania and Texas, are anticipated to generate nearly 10 gigawatts of power. This substantial capacity highlights the growing necessity for dependable energy sources, especially as AI-driven data centers increasingly require vast and reliable electricity supplies. While customer details for these projects remain undisclosed, the scale of investment underscores the swift transformation in power development driven by data center demand.

NextEra Energy's regulated utility, Florida Power & Light (FPL), is currently experiencing a surge in demand, with its pipeline of data-center requests expanding to 21 gigawatts. The company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with expectations for these projects to become operational by 2028. In addition to these initiatives, NextEra is also working to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa, which is intended to provide power for Google data centers.

Moreover, the company is not solely focused on traditional power generation. NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects to its portfolio, bringing the total backlog to approximately 33 gigawatts. This diversified approach positions NextEra as a leader in the energy sector, capable of meeting the diverse needs of modern data centers while continuing to invest in sustainable energy solutions.

In its recent financial performance, NextEra Energy reported an adjusted earnings per share of $1.09 for the first quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $0.96. As the company continues to innovate and adapt to the demands of the energy market, it remains a focal point for investors looking at utility stocks. While there are opportunities in artificial intelligence stocks, the potential of NextEra Energy as a long-term investment cannot be overlooked.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 290 words

Sun May 3, 2026: NextEra Energy Charts Course in Utility Sector's AI-Driven Future

NextEra Energy is leading the transformation of utility stocks as the demand for electricity surges due to the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers. On 4th May 2026, the emergence of AI technology since 2022 has highlighted critical differences in how power providers manage grid strain and infrastructure readiness.

Investors are increasingly distinguishing between companies prepared for this surge and those lagging behind. NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) stands out as a primary example of a utility firm strategically positioned to thrive in this high-demand environment through modernization and expansion.

NextEra Energy, formerly recognized as Florida Power & Light, continues to provide electricity to 12 million residents in Florida. However, the company has evolved into one of the largest energy wholesalers in the United States, operating with 81 gigawatts of power production capacity. The company maintains ambitious growth plans, with the potential to approximately double its current output by the year 2032.

Beyond simple capacity, NextEra Energy is actively rethinking the operational framework of the electric utility sector. A major component of this strategy involves direct collaboration with major technology firms to address specific infrastructure needs. In December of last year, the company established a partnership with Alphabet's Google, focusing on designing and constructing data centers that are specifically engineered to be both power-efficient and self-sufficient from the ground up.

The company is also developing grid optimization solutions powered by AI to manage resources more effectively. NextEra Energy aims to establish 15 gigawatts of new production capacity specifically dedicated to data center hubs by 2035. These proactive measures coincide with projections suggesting that US data center power demand could grow 30-fold between 2024 and 2035, positioning NextEra Energy as a key player in negotiating favorable agreements in the evolving utility landscape.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 349 words

Sat May 2, 2026: Salt River Project Partners with NextEra Energy Resources for Major Solar Initiative

Salt River Project has announced a transformative power purchase agreement with NextEra Energy Resources, marking a significant step in the utility's renewable energy strategy. The deal, revealed on 4th May 2026, encompasses the development of 3,000 MW of solar generation alongside 1,000 MW of battery storage capacity, with construction slated to take place across Arizona and completion expected by 2027.

This initiative aligns with Salt River Project's broader objective to phase out coal-fired power generation by 2032. The solar and storage capacities combined will provide electricity for approximately 675,000 homes at peak output. Notably, the projects will utilize bifacial solar modules and tracking systems, which are designed to maximize energy capture in the challenging desert Southwest climate.

The 1,000 MW of battery storage is strategically aimed at addressing the evening peak period when solar production wanes while household energy demand rises. By storing solar energy for later use, Salt River Project intends to minimize its reliance on natural gas plants, thereby enhancing grid stability during periods of extreme heat. Furthermore, the utility anticipates that this significant buildout will not only generate tax revenue for local communities but also create numerous construction jobs over the next three years.

As of early 2026, Arizona has exceeded 11.4 GW of installed solar capacity, according to data from the Solar Energy Industries Association, with over 9,700 individuals employed in the state's solar sector. Solar power currently represents about 16% of Arizona's electricity mix, with expectations to add an additional 13.8 GW of capacity within the next five years. This expansion is indicative of the state's commitment to renewable energy and its potential for further growth in the sector.

In conjunction with this agreement, a comprehensive report on the solar cells and light-emitting diodes industry in the United States provides valuable insights into market dynamics, tracking demand, supply, and trade flows. The report benchmarks prices, margins, and trade routes, offering critical data for stakeholders in the solar landscape. This information is designed to aid manufacturers, distributors, and investors in navigating the evolving solar market and making informed decisions as they plan for the future.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Nextera Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is a clean energy utility with notable achievements in the renewable energy sector, spotlighted in recent NextEra Energy stock news. As an energy storage developer, NextEra significantly increases its energy generation capacity through strategic investments in NextEra wind power projects, solar power projects, and battery storage projects. Recent analyst upgrades, such as the NEE analyst upgrade $8, have led to the NEE price target increase, as Analyst ups NextEra Energy price target, prompting a positive response from NextEra Energy investor news. With its robust NextEra capital expenditure plan and commitment to carbon reduction initiatives, NextEra infrastructure projects and project pipeline reflect its long-term growth outlook. The company’s focus on utility-scale solar projects and offshore wind enhances its NextEra ESG performance. Analysts have responded to this promising trajectory, as evidenced by the NextEra Energy price target raised updates, highlighting their impact on renewable energy stocks NEE. Florida Power & Light (FPL) and NextEra Energy Resources are pivotal in expanding NextEra power purchase agreements and dividend yield.
